mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of declining rate periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by >0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or returns are negative in >50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rising production periods when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained labor market strength and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 36-month rolling window, or 60-day returns are negative in >50% of positive payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between sluggish real GDP growth and contracting personal consumption expenditures signals a demand-side recessionary shock, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.5% while RSAFS declines
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >55% of instances where both GDP growth and consumption expenditures decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaking above its long-term trend signals an acceleration in manufacturing activity and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average by >1.5%
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of trend-break instances over a 24-month walk-forward test.
